# XFOIL hard: two viscous polars

Wrap the `xfoil` executable and save two viscous polar files at different Reynolds numbers when XFOIL is installed.

## Why This Case Exists

A harder XFOIL challenge should prove more than one polar sweep by crossing Reynolds numbers and retaining both saved outputs.

## Case Shape

- ID: `xfoil_hard`
- Family: `xfoil`
- Difficulty: `hard`
- Tier: `local_full`
- Style: `command`
- Workflow Slice: `aerodynamics`
- Target Kind: `command`
- Upstream Target: `xfoil`
- Catalog Source: `challenges/cases/xfoil_hard/challenge.toml`

## Ingestion Breadth

This case relies on its workflow without extra breadth assertions.

## Why This Stays A Challenge

This case keeps its ingestion recipe and workflow assertions in
`challenge.toml` instead of checking in companion `ingest.py` and
`use.py` scripts. That boundary is intentional: the challenge lane
should stay a compact problem statement rather than turning into a
worked answer key like the case-study lane.

## Run This Case

Use the convenience target:

```bash
make challenge CASE=xfoil_hard
```

Or call the runner directly:

```bash
PYTHONPATH=src .venv/bin/python scripts/run_challenges.py --catalog-dir challenges/cases --tier all --only xfoil_hard
```

## Prepared Inputs

This case does not need rendered setup inputs.

## Fixtures

This case does not need checked-in fixtures.

## Workflow

### 1. Generate two viscous polar files at two Reynolds numbers

Tool: `run_xfoil`

Arguments:

```json
{
  "cwd": "{challenge_artifact_dir}",
  "stdin_text": "NACA 0012\nPANE\nPLOP\nG\n\nOPER\nVISC 200000\nALFA -2\nPACC\npolar_re200k.txt\n\nASEQ -2 8 0.5\nPACC\nRE 150000\nINIT\nALFA -2\nPACC\npolar_re150k.txt\n\nASEQ -2 8 0.5\nPACC\n\nQUIT\n"
}
```

Expectations: json fields {'exit_code': 0}; files are non-empty ['polar_re200k.txt', 'polar_re150k.txt'].

## What This Case Proves

- A deterministic wrapper can drive more than one XFOIL polar-generation sequence in one batch session.
- The challenge harness can retain multiple real aerodynamic outputs from a classic interactive CLI.

## Known Limits

- This case intentionally reports `skipped_unavailable` when `xfoil` is not installed.

## Challenge Definition

```toml
# Self-contained case: step up to two saved viscous XFOIL polars.
# This hard rung follows the official batch-session pattern for multiple Reynolds numbers.
id = "xfoil_hard"
title = "XFOIL hard: two viscous polars"
tier = "local_full"
style = "command"
slice = "aerodynamics"
family = "xfoil"
difficulty = "hard"

[example]
summary = "Wrap the `xfoil` executable and save two viscous polar files at different Reynolds numbers when XFOIL is installed."
motivation = "A harder XFOIL challenge should prove more than one polar sweep by crossing Reynolds numbers and retaining both saved outputs."
proves = [
  "A deterministic wrapper can drive more than one XFOIL polar-generation sequence in one batch session.",
  "The challenge harness can retain multiple real aerodynamic outputs from a classic interactive CLI.",
]
limitations = [
  "This case intentionally reports `skipped_unavailable` when `xfoil` is not installed.",
]

[target]
kind = "command"
value = ["xfoil"]

[probe]
commands = [["xfoil"]]

[scaffold]
kind = "command"
function_name = "run_xfoil"
help_probe_args = ["-h"]

[workflow]
[[workflow.steps]]
label = "Generate two viscous polar files at two Reynolds numbers"
tool = "run_xfoil"

[workflow.steps.arguments]
cwd = "{challenge_artifact_dir}"
stdin_text = """
NACA 0012
PANE
PLOP
G

OPER
VISC 200000
ALFA -2
PACC
polar_re200k.txt

ASEQ -2 8 0.5
PACC
RE 150000
INIT
ALFA -2
PACC
polar_re150k.txt

ASEQ -2 8 0.5
PACC

QUIT
"""

[workflow.steps.expect]
json_fields = { exit_code = 0 }
files_nonempty = ["polar_re200k.txt", "polar_re150k.txt"]
```
